title: How to Deal with Spiritual Attachment
meta_description: Discover the truth about spiritual attachment. It's not demons or possession. Learn to identify and release stuck energy from people, places, and memories.
og_title: How to Deal with Spiritual Attachment
og_description: Discover the truth about spiritual attachment. It's not demons or possession. Learn to identify and release stuck energy from people, places, and memories.
body:
Spiritual Attachment Isn't What You Think It Is

Let's cut right to it. When you hear "spiritual attachment," your mind probably goes straight to horror movies. Demonic possession. Ghosts clinging to your aura. Something dark and terrifying that needs to be screamed out of you by someone with a holy book and a strong opinion about your soul.

That's not what I'm talking about. Not even close.

Spiritual attachment, in the way I work with it, is something far more ordinary and far more common. It's the energetic residue of a person, a place, a memory, or a belief that gets stuck in your field. It's not a demon. It's not a curse. It's just... stuff. Sticky stuff that doesn't belong to you.

And if you're the kind of person who feels everything all the time - who walks into a room and instantly knows who's fighting, who's sad, who's hiding something - you're probably carrying more of this stuff than you realize. Right?!

You didn't ask for it. You didn't invite it. But your nervous system is wide open, like a radio picking up every station at once. That's not a flaw. That's how you're built. The problem is, nobody taught you how to change the channel.

So let's talk about what spiritual attachment actually looks like in real life, and more importantly, what you can do about it.

What Spiritual Attachment Actually Feels Like (Your Experience, Not the Movies)

You don't need to see ghosts to have spiritual attachments. You just need to notice patterns in your life that don't make sense. Like:

  • You suddenly feel exhausted around a specific person, even though you just woke up
  • You take on other people's emotions like they're your own - you feel their anger, their grief, their anxiety, and you can't shake it
  • You have thoughts that don't feel like yours, especially negative ones about yourself that came out of nowhere
  • You feel heavy, dense, or like there's a weight pressing on your chest or shoulders
  • You walk into a room and instantly feel sick, anxious, or drained for no reason
  • You keep having the same arguments with yourself - loops of shame, guilt, or fear that you can't seem to finish

Does that land? Because that's the real face of spiritual attachment. It's not dramatic. It's not cinematic. It's just... exhausting. It's the feeling of carrying something that isn't yours, day after day, without knowing how to put it down.

And here's the thing - most people who experience this have been told their whole lives that they're too sensitive. Too emotional. Too dramatic. Too much. They start to believe there's something wrong with them. They try to shut it down, numb it out, or build walls so thick that nothing can get through.

But that's like trying to fix a radio by breaking the antenna. You don't need to stop receiving. You need to learn how to tune.

"The wound that makes you feel everything is the same wound that lets you heal everything. You don't need to close it. You need to learn how to work with it."

Where Spiritual Attachments Come From (It's Not What You Think)

Most spiritual attachments don't come from dark entities or malevolent spirits. They come from everyday life. Here are the most common sources I've seen in my work:

1. People You're Close To

This is the big one. When you love someone deeply - a partner, a parent, a child, a best friend - your energy fields start to merge. That's natural. That's connection. The problem is when their pain, their trauma, or their emotional patterns get stuck in your field and don't leave. You start carrying their anxiety. Their depression. Their fear of abandonment. And you don't even realize it's not yours.

2. Places With History

Every room, every building, every piece of land holds memory. If you're sensitive, you walk into a space and you feel it. The old argument that happened in that kitchen. The grief that soaked into the walls of that bedroom. The joy that still lingers in the living room of a childhood home. That's not your stuff. But if you don't know how to clear it, it sticks to you like smoke.

3. Traumatic Events (Yours or Someone Else's)

Trauma leaves a mark. When you go through something overwhelming, part of your energy can get stuck in that moment. You're still living in the past, energetically speaking. And if you're an empath, you can also pick up fragments of other people's trauma - especially if you were present when it happened, or if you're close to someone who's carrying it.

4. Beliefs You Absorbed Without Choosing

This one is sneaky. You grew up in a family, a culture, a religion that had certain ideas about who you are and what you deserve. "You're not good enough." "You have to earn love." "It's selfish to put yourself first." Those aren't your thoughts. They're attachments. They're programs that got installed in your field, and they run in the background, shaping your life without your permission.

How to Tell If You're Carrying Something That Isn't Yours

Here's a simple test. Ask yourself these questions:

  • Does this feeling make sense given what's happening right now?
  • Does this thought feel like it came from me, or does it feel like it was dropped into my head?
  • When I'm alone, do I feel lighter, calmer, more like myself?
  • Do I feel different around different people - not just emotionally, but physically?
  • Do I have a hard time knowing what I actually want, because I'm so tuned into what everyone else wants?

If you answered yes to most of these, you're probably carrying attachments. And that's not your fault. But it is your responsibility to start clearing them. Because here's the truth: you can't be fully yourself when you're full of everyone else.

How to Clear Spiritual Attachments (Practical Steps That Work)

I'm not going to tell you to burn sage and call it done. Sage is great. I love sage. But if you don't address the energetic patterns that keep drawing attachments to you, you'll just be clearing the same stuff over and over. That's not healing. That's housekeeping.

Real clearing requires two things: awareness and boundaries. You need to know what's yours and what isn't, and you need to be willing to say no to what isn't yours.

Step 1: Get Still and Feel What's Actually There

You can't clear what you can't feel. So start by slowing down. Sit somewhere quiet. Close your eyes. Take a few breaths. And then just notice: what am I carrying right now? Don't try to figure it out. Don't analyze. Just feel. Is there heaviness in your chest? A knot in your stomach? Pressure behind your eyes? That's information. That's the attachment showing itself.

A good book to help you with this is The Psychic Empath by Rose Rosetree. It walks you through how to sense what's in your field and what isn't. See on Amazon (paid link)

Step 2: Name What Doesn't Belong to You

Once you feel the heaviness, say it out loud. "This anxiety is not mine." "This shame is not mine." "This belief that I'm not good enough is not mine." Don't argue with it. Don't try to figure out where it came from. Just name it. You're drawing a line in the sand. You're telling your field: I see you, and you don't belong here.

Step 3: Breathe It Out

This sounds too simple, but it works. Take a slow breath in. As you exhale, imagine the attachment leaving your field like smoke leaving a room. You can visualize it as gray mist, as threads being cut, as a weight lifting off your shoulders. Do this three to five times. Notice how your body feels afterward. Lighter? More spacious? That's the attachment leaving.

Step 4: Call Your Energy Back

Most attachments happen because you left pieces of yourself somewhere - in a past relationship, in a traumatic moment, in a place where you gave too much. So call it back. Say this, out loud or in your mind: "I call back all the energy I've left behind in any person, place, time, or experience. I reclaim my energy now, fully and completely. I am whole." Feel it coming back into your body. Feel yourself filling up.

Step 5: Set a Boundary in Your Field

This is the most important step. If you don't set a boundary, you'll just pick up more attachments tomorrow. So imagine a protective layer around your body - like a bubble, a shield, or a filter. It doesn't block connection. It just filters out what isn't yours. Tell yourself: "I am open to love, to connection, to the world. But I only take in what serves me and what is mine to carry. Everything else passes through me without sticking."

Why Some Attachments Keep Coming Back (And What to Do About It)

If you clear an attachment and it returns, that's a sign that something deeper is going on. Usually, it's one of two things:

1. You have a pattern of giving your power away. Maybe you were taught that your needs don't matter. Maybe you learned that love means losing yourself. Maybe you're afraid of being alone, so you absorb other people's energy to feel connected. If that's the case, clearing the attachment is like wiping the counter while the faucet is still running. You need to address the underlying pattern.

2. You're connected to someone who's actively projecting onto you. Some people - especially family members, partners, or people you're very close to - unconsciously dump their energy onto you. They don't mean to. They're just not aware of what they're doing. In that case, you need to strengthen your boundaries and, in some cases, create physical or emotional distance until you're strong enough to hold your ground.

If you're dealing with deep patterns like this, I highly recommend Becoming Supernatural by Dr. Joe Dispenza. It has some incredible techniques for rewiring the nervous system and breaking old patterns. See on Amazon (paid link)

The Role of Your Nervous System in Spiritual Attachment

Here's something most people don't talk about: your nervous system is the gateway for attachments. When you're in a state of chronic stress, fear, or hypervigilance, your field gets porous. You leak energy. You pick up energy. You become a sponge for everything around you.

That's why grounding is so important. Grounding isn't just a woo-woo concept. It's a physiological reset. When you ground, you bring your awareness into your body, into the present moment, into the earth beneath you. That calms your nervous system. And when your nervous system is calm, your field becomes more coherent. Attachments have a harder time sticking.

Try this right now: feel your feet on the floor. Really feel them. Notice the pressure. The temperature. The texture of your socks or the floor. Take a breath and imagine roots growing from the soles of your feet down into the earth. Let yourself be held. Let yourself be heavy. That's grounding. Do this every day, especially before and after you're in crowded or stressful situations.

Another great resource for nervous system regulation is The Body Keeps the Score by Bessel van der Kolk. It's not a spiritual book, but it's essential reading for anyone who wants to understand how trauma lives in the body. See on Amazon (paid link)

When to Seek Help (And What Kind of Help Actually Works)

Most spiritual attachments can be cleared on your own. But sometimes, you need help. Here's when to reach out:

  • You've tried clearing multiple times and the attachment keeps coming back
  • You feel like something is actively draining you, especially at night
  • You have thoughts that feel violent, intrusive, or completely out of character
  • You feel like you're losing yourself - like you don't know who you are anymore
  • You're dealing with addiction, severe depression, or chronic illness that doesn't respond to treatment

If any of that is true for you, find someone who works with energy clearing, shamanic extraction, or soul retrieval. Look for someone who has experience and who doesn't make you feel like a victim. The goal isn't to be saved. The goal is to be reminded of your own strength.

And if you're looking for a practical, step-by-step guide to clearing and protecting your energy, I highly recommend The Art of Psychic Protection by Judy Hall. It's full of techniques you can use in everyday life. See on Amazon (paid link)

What Life Looks Like Without the Attachments

I want you to imagine something for a second. Imagine waking up and feeling only your own feelings. Imagine walking into a room and not absorbing everyone's mood. Imagine knowing what you want, not because you've figured it out, but because you can actually hear your own voice over the noise.

That's not a fantasy. That's what's possible when you start clearing attachments and building energetic boundaries. You don't lose your sensitivity. You actually gain more of it - because you're no longer drowning in everyone else's stuff. You can finally feel what's yours. And that's a beautiful thing.

You'll still feel the world. You'll still be moved by other people's pain. You'll still care deeply. But you won't be consumed by it. You'll be able to hold space for others without losing yourself. You'll be able to love without merging. You'll be able to walk through crowded rooms and come out the other side still feeling like you.

That's the goal. Not to be numb. Not to be disconnected. To be fully, completely, unapologetically yourself - in a world that never taught you how.

You can learn. You're already learning. And you're not broken. You never were.

If this article connected with you, I'd encourage you to take our free assessment to see where you are on your spiritual awakening journey. It's a quick quiz that helps you understand your current stage and what you need most right now. Take the assessment here or check out our quiz page for more tools to support your path.
